goal: UTMB CCC (101km, 6100m+) in late August 2026 — finish under 20 hours

weeks.focus: Re-establish aerobic base. Easy effort dominates.

workouts.name: Rest
workouts.description: Full rest. Walk if you like.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Flat or rolling. Conversational pace — you should be able to nose-breathe.

workouts.name: Hill strides
workouts.description: 30 min easy, then 6x30s uphill at hard effort, 90s walk down. 10 min cool down. Builds neuromuscular power without taxing systems.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Trails if available. Keep heart rate Z2.

workouts.name: Rest or cross
workouts.description: Optional easy spin, swim, or strength. Active recovery only.

workouts.name: Long run
workouts.description: Trail with ~600m vertical if possible. Time on feet matters more than pace. Walk steep climbs.

workouts.name: Recovery jog
workouts.description: Very easy shakeout. Just loosen the legs.

workouts.name: Rest
workouts.description: Full rest.

workouts.name: Easy + strides
workouts.description: 45 min easy, then 4x20s strides at 5k effort with full recovery.

workouts.name: Tempo
workouts.description: 15 min warm up, 25 min at comfortably-hard (half-marathon effort), 15 min cool down. First quality session — controlled.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Trail or road. Z2.

workouts.name: Cross train
workouts.description: Strength session: squats, lunges, single-leg work. Critical for mountain running.

workouts.name: Long run
workouts.description: Trail with 700-800m vertical. Practice fueling — 60g carbs/hour. Walk steep climbs at zone-2 HR.

workouts.name: Recovery jog
workouts.description: Easy shakeout, trails if possible.

weeks.focus: Push the long run further. Back-to-back weekend.

workouts.name: Rest
workouts.description: Full rest.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Trail preferred. Easy effort.

workouts.name: Hill intervals
workouts.description: 15 min warm up, 5x3 min uphill at hard effort with jog-down recovery, 10 min cool down. Find a 6-10% gradient.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Recovery between hard days. Strictly easy.

workouts.name: Strength
workouts.description: Lower-body + core. Bulgarian splits, step-ups, planks.

workouts.name: Long run
workouts.description: Trail with 1000m vertical. Race-day fueling practice. Back-to-back tomorrow.

workouts.name: Medium run
workouts.description: Run on tired legs — this is the magic for ultra prep. Very easy effort, hike if needed.

weeks.focus: Recovery week. ~30% volume drop. Absorb the training.

workouts.name: Rest
workouts.description: Full rest.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Short and very easy.

workouts.name: Easy + strides
workouts.description: 35 min easy, 4x20s strides. Keep legs sharp without depth.

workouts.name: Rest
workouts.description: Active recovery — walk, yoga, swim.

workouts.name: Easy run
workouts.description: Easy effort, trails.

workouts.name: Long run
workouts.description: Cut long run by half. Z2 throughout, no vertical focus.

workouts.name: Recovery jog
workouts.description: Easy shakeout. You should feel restored by Monday.

notes: Sample 4-week excerpt from a longer build. Real plan would run 16-24 weeks. This block shows a classic 3:1 cycle (3 build weeks, 1 recovery), with the long run progressing 22 → 25 → 28 km before dropping to 14 km. Hard sessions are flanked by easy days. Strength on Friday is non-negotiable for mountain races — quad strength dictates how well you descend after 80 km.
